name: Weekly risk monitoring report for 40 active risks
description: Validates the Risk Report Generator can produce a comprehensive weekly report for a project with 40 active risks across all categories, including heat map, top-10 ranking, and reserve burn-down.
prompt: Generate a weekly risk monitoring report for the CloudMigrate project (Sprint 14, March 10-14 2026). The project has 40 active risks: 12 technical, 10 organizational, 8 external, and 10 project management. This sprint: 3 new risks identified, 2 risks closed, 1 risk triggered (R-017: key vendor missed SLA). Risk reserve started at $450K, $280K remaining. SPI is 0.91 and CPI is 0.88. The top risk is R-003 (data migration integrity failure, P=High, I=Catastrophic, exposure=20). Include the full heat map, top-10 list, and reserve burn-down showing the last 4 sprints.
expected_output_patterns: heat map with 5x5 probability-impact matrix
top-10 risks ranked by exposure score
3 newly identified risks with source attribution
2 closed risks with resolution summary
triggered risk R-017 with contingency activation status
risk reserve burn-down table with 4 sprint history
reserve forecast for remaining project duration
actions required section with owners and deadlines

name: Risk trigger activation when SPI drops below 0.85
description: Validates the Trigger Monitor correctly detects an SPI threshold breach, classifies severity, and activates the linked contingency plan with proper notifications.
prompt: The FinTech Platform Modernization project has risk R-029 defined as: 'Schedule overrun on payment gateway module exceeding 15% of baseline.' Trigger condition: SPI < 0.85 for the payment gateway work package. The latest earned value data shows SPI dropped from 0.87 last week to 0.82 this week. The linked contingency plan is 'CONT-029: Fast-track critical path by adding 2 senior developers and compressing testing phases with automated regression suite.' Risk owner is Maria Chen (Tech Lead). PM is David Park. Response SLA is 48 hours. Process this trigger breach and generate the full alert.
expected_output_patterns: alert classification as TRIGGERED or CRITICAL
breach details with threshold 0.85 vs actual 0.82
breach magnitude calculation
contingency plan CONT-029 activation
Maria Chen and David Park notification records
48-hour response SLA documented
specific actions with owners and deadlines
trigger history showing previous week value 0.87

name: Trend analysis showing increasing risk exposure over 3 sprints
description: Validates the Risk Trend Analyst can detect and articulate an increasing risk exposure trajectory across 3 sprints with proper metrics, segmentation, and actionable recommendations.
prompt: Analyze the risk trends for the HealthConnect EHR Integration project over Sprints 8, 9, and 10. Data: Sprint 8 had 22 active risks (exposure score 145), Sprint 9 had 28 active risks (exposure score 178), Sprint 10 has 35 active risks (exposure score 215). New risks per sprint: 5, 8, 11. Closed per sprint: 3, 2, 4. Average time-to-mitigation went from 12 days to 15 days to 19 days. Mitigation success rate dropped from 78% to 71% to 65%. Technical risks are the fastest-growing category (8, 12, 18). Risk reserve is at 55% with 40% of project remaining. The risk appetite threshold is exposure score 250. Produce the full trend analysis.
expected_output_patterns: exposure trend direction identified as Increasing
acceleration identified as Accelerating
forecast projecting next-period exposure score
time-to-risk-appetite-breach calculation against 250 threshold
metrics dashboard with all 3 sprints compared
technical category flagged as highest growth concern
mitigation success rate decline highlighted as concern
reserve consumption vs project completion gap identified
3 specific concerns with data evidence
actionable recommendations for strategy adjustment

name: New risk identification from external regulatory change
description: Validates the Risk Radar Scanner can detect and properly classify a new risk signal from an external regulatory change, correlate it with existing project risks, and escalate appropriately.
prompt: The DataVault Analytics Platform project is in Phase 3 (data pipeline development). The Risk Radar Scanner detects the following external signal: the EU has announced GDPR Amendment 2026.3 effective July 1, requiring all cross-border data processing to implement real-time consent verification. The project currently processes data across 4 EU member states and the architecture assumes batch consent verification (daily). Additionally, the schedule shows the data pipeline module is already 8% behind baseline (SPI 0.92), and the team lead for the compliance module resigned last week. Scan these signals, identify correlations, and produce the radar report with draft risk register entries.
expected_output_patterns: GDPR Amendment 2026.3 identified as external regulatory signal
correlation between regulatory change and existing architecture gap
correlation with compliance team lead resignation
compound risk assessment across all 3 signals
severity scoring above threshold for escalation
draft risk register entry with probability and impact
classification as external/regulatory risk category
recommended risk owner assignment
suggested response strategy addressing real-time consent requirement
schedule impact assessment given existing SPI 0.92
